heading: Annual reporting on drug shortages
label: 356c–1 Annual reporting on drug shortages

children.text: specifies the number of manufacturers that submitted a notification to the Secretary under during such calendar year;section 356c(a) of this title

describes the communication between the field investigators of the Food and Drug Administration and the staff of the Center for Drug Evaluation and Research’s Office of Compliance and Drug Shortage Program, including the Food and Drug Administration’s procedures for enabling and ensuring such communication;
describes the coordination and alignment activities undertaken pursuant to ;section 356d(g) of this title

provides the number of reports that were required under to be sent to the appropriate offices of the Food and Drug Administration with expertise regarding drug shortages, and the number of such reports that were sent;section 374(b)(2) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: lists the major actions taken by the Secretary to prevent or mitigate the drug shortages described in paragraph (9);

children.label: i
children.text: the number of applications and supplements for which the Secretary expedited review under during such calendar year; andsection 356c(g)(1) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of establishment inspections or reinspections that the Secretary expedited under during such calendar year;section 356c(g)(2) of this title
describes the coordination between the Food and Drug Administration and the Drug Enforcement Administration on efforts to prevent or alleviate drug shortages;
identifies the number of and describes the instances in which the Food and Drug Administration exercised regulatory flexibility and discretion to prevent or alleviate a drug shortage;
lists the names of manufacturers that were issued letters under ; andsection 356c(f) of this title
specifies the number of drug shortages occurring during such calendar year, as identified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Trend analysis
children.label: b Trend analysis
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to retain a third party to conduct a study, if the Secretary believes such a study would help clarify the causes, trends, or solutions related to drug shortages.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: c Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “drug shortage” or “shortage” has the meaning given such term in .section 356c of this title
